title: Modifying Strings
description: Change text by modifying strings like arrays.

slides.title: Changing String Characters 🔧
slides.content: Because strings act like arrays, we can also overwrite individual characters inside the string using their index.

slides.title: Example

quizData.question: When modifying a single character in a string like myStr[0], you MUST use single quotes (' ') around the new character.
quizData.explanation: Yes. A string is an array of chars. Notice that we used 'J' (single quotes), not "J" (double quotes). Double quotes denote a full string, not a single char.

slides.title: Special Characters 🚨
slides.content:
Because strings must be written within quotes, C++ will get confused if you try to put quotes INSIDE your string.

`string txt = "We are the so-called "Vikings" from the north.";`
This will cause a compiling error!

slides.title: Escape Characters 🏃‍♂️
slides.content:
To fix this, we use the backslash `\` escape character. It turns special characters into printable string characters.

`string txt = "We are the so-called \"Vikings\" from the north.";`

You can also use `\n` to insert a new line.

quizData.question: Which of the following creates a new line in a string?
quizData.explanation: The \n character represents a 'newline' command in C++ strings.
